title: Strengthen _patch_stage meta-tests: isinstance checks, postconditions, cross-assertions
analysis:
The _patch_stage helper in TestProjectIdValidation (test_stages.py, lines 408-429) is a context manager that patches two things: stage.assemble_payload (via patch.object) and base_module.run_stage_via_cli (via patch). Two meta-tests exist (lines 568-591) that verify the helper works. Three weaknesses need fixing:

1. Line 576 uses `hasattr(stage.assemble_payload, 'side_effect')` — any object with a side_effect attr would pass. Need `isinstance(..., (AsyncMock, MagicMock))` which is the canonical mock check.

2. Neither meta-test checks that the context manager restores state on exit. The `with` blocks end without postcondition assertions. After exit, base_module.run_stage_via_cli should be the original function and stage.assemble_payload should no longer be a mock.

3. test_patch_stage_accepts_cli_side_effect (line 580) only checks the CLI side_effect wiring but never verifies that assemble_payload is also patched — it should cross-assert both patches are active regardless of which parameter path was taken.

All changes are confined to test_stages.py. MagicMock must be added to the import on line 5. No production code changes needed.

prerequisites.description: Add MagicMock to the unittest.mock import on line 5 of test_stages.py: `from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch`

steps.description: In test_patch_stage_patches_assemble_payload_and_run_stage: replace `assert hasattr(stage.assemble_payload, 'side_effect')` (line 576) with `assert isinstance(stage.assemble_payload, (AsyncMock, MagicMock))`. Run the test to verify it passes — the patched assemble_payload is an AsyncMock which satisfies isinstance.

In test_patch_stage_patches_assemble_payload_and_run_stage: add postcondition assertions AFTER the `with self._patch_stage(stage):` block exits. Save `original_assemble_payload = stage.assemble_payload` before the with block. After the block, assert: (a) `base_module.run_stage_via_cli is original_run_stage_via_cli` — CLI function restored, (b) `not isinstance(stage.assemble_payload, (AsyncMock, MagicMock))` — assemble_payload is no longer a mock, (c) `stage.assemble_payload is original_assemble_payload` — exact original method reference restored.

In test_patch_stage_accepts_cli_side_effect: add `assert isinstance(stage.assemble_payload, (AsyncMock, MagicMock))` inside the `with` block (before or after the existing side_effect assertion). This cross-asserts that both patches are active even when the cli_side_effect parameter path is exercised.
Run the full TestProjectIdValidation test class (`pytest fused-memory/tests/test_stages.py::TestProjectIdValidation -v`) to verify all meta-tests pass and no existing tests regressed.

design_decisions.decision: Use isinstance(..., (AsyncMock, MagicMock)) tuple check rather than just AsyncMock
design_decisions.rationale: patch.object with side_effect creates a MagicMock wrapping an async side_effect. Depending on the mock library version and patch mode, the resulting object may be AsyncMock or MagicMock. Checking both covers all cases and matches the task spec.

design_decisions.decision: Save original_assemble_payload reference before the with block for exact restoration check
design_decisions.rationale: Checking `not isinstance(..., Mock)` is necessary but not sufficient — it doesn't prove the original method was restored vs some other non-mock object. Comparing identity with the saved reference gives a stronger guarantee.

design_decisions.decision: Only modify TestProjectIdValidation meta-tests, not TestRunIdValidation
design_decisions.rationale: The task description specifically targets TestProjectIdValidation lines ~568-591. TestRunIdValidation has no equivalent meta-tests to strengthen. The _patch_stage helper implementations are identical between the two classes.

design_decisions.decision: Group all test changes into one commit rather than one-per-assertion
design_decisions.rationale: All three improvements are tightly coupled to the same two test methods. They form a single logical change — 'strengthen meta-tests' — and splitting would create noisy git history with no benefit.

reuse.how: No modification needed to the helper itself — only its meta-tests are being strengthened
MagicMock is added to the same import line; AsyncMock is already used throughout the file
The existing pattern of saving original_run_stage_via_cli before the with block and comparing identity after is reused for the postcondition check
